companies.name: Oracle Corporation
companies.reason: Oracle is trending following a massive earnings beat and the announcement of a groundbreaking partnership to bring its database services to Amazon Web Services (AWS). Chairman Larry Ellison’s comments about investing in AI 'superclusters' powered by nuclear reactors have also captured market attention regarding AI infrastructure demand.

companies.name: Palantir Technologies Inc.
companies.reason: Palantir is seeing a surge in interest after the announcement of its addition to the S&P 500 index. Its shift from a government contractor to a mainstream commercial AI provider, driven by its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) boot camps, is a key driver for its current growth narrative.

companies.name: Apple Inc.
companies.reason: Apple is in the spotlight following the launch of the iPhone 16 and 'Apple Intelligence.' Investors are watching for a potential upgrade 'super-cycle,' while the European Court of Justice ruling that Apple must pay $14.4 billion in back taxes adds regulatory focus.

companies.name: Adobe Inc.
companies.reason: Adobe is trending due to its aggressive expansion into AI-generated video with the 'Firefly Video Model,' aiming to compete with OpenAI’s Sora. It is positioning itself as a commercially safe alternative for AI content creation.

companies.name: Arm Holdings plc
companies.reason: Arm is back in focus as its architecture powers the AI-centric chips in the new iPhone 16. The use of the latest V9 architecture, which commands higher royalty rates, positions Arm as a primary beneficiary of the shift toward 'Edge AI.'

companies.name: Broadcom Inc.
companies.reason: Broadcom is a major focus for its dominance in AI networking and custom AI accelerators (ASICs) for hyperscalers like Google and Meta. It is currently the leading 'non-GPU' AI infrastructure play in the semiconductor sector.

companies.name: AppLovin Corporation
companies.reason: AppLovin has gained significant attention for its AI-driven ad-tech monetization success with the AXON 2.0 engine. It has become a key case study for companies successfully translating AI software capabilities into significant revenue growth.
